calendar.matters:
Petition To Appoint Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Sep-06-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nancy Levin for University of Minnesota Foundation.
Kathleen Newsom (nominated Successor Trustee) also present.

Ms. Levin addressed the issue of due diligence in finding Mr. Roberts stating that they found that two out of the three named Trustees had died, that there were many Gerald Roberts in the Bay Area, that Mr. Roberts had been a Conservator for one of the Beneficiaries which lead them to believe this was the right one, that they wrote a letter to him and that there was no response. Ms. Levin also stated that Mr. Larson's declaration talks about how they found him, spoke of communications they had with a bank and trust company, stated that none of the banker trustees will take it because of the amount of money, and that she and Mr. Schatz agreed on a Professional Fiduciary. In addition, Ms. Levin addressed her fees stating that her Client is a 50% Beneficiary, that if $11,500.00 comes out of the trust, $5,750.00, would have gone to them (Client) anyway, spoke of time spent in providing her services, stated that she reduced her hours and asked the Court to cross out the words "up to an additional" in the order. The Court indicated that it only needs to cross out the words "up to". The Court granted the Petition to Appoint Successor Trustee (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
